fr:title.text: Creation of colimits by the forgetful functor ›

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Creation of colimits by the forgetful functor ›

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let {\mathcal {I}}\xrightarrow {{X_\bullet }}{\mathcal {C}^{\mathbb {T}}} be a diagram such that \mathcal {I}\xrightarrow {X_\bullet }\mathcal {C}^{\mathbb {T}}\xrightarrow {U}\mathcal {C} has a (co)limit cocone {UX_\bullet }\xrightarrow {{c_\bullet }}{{\mathopen {}\left \{C\right \}\mathclose {}}}. Let \bar {C}={\mathopen {}\left (C,\alpha _{\bar {C}}\right )\mathclose {}} be the canonical algebra structure on the colimit apex C such that {UX_\bullet }\xrightarrow {{c_\bullet }}{{\mathopen {}\left \{C\right \}\mathclose {}}} lifts to a cocone of algebras. Then we conclude that this cocone is universal because U reflects colimits of \mathcal {I}-figures. It is evident (by definition) that U preserves this colimit.

fr:title.text: Creation of colimits by the forgetful functor

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Creation of colimits by the forgetful functor

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \mathcal {C} be a category and let \mathbb {T} = {\mathopen {}\left (T,\eta ,\mu \right )\mathclose {}} be a monad on \mathcal {C}, writing {\mathcal {C}^{\mathbb {T}}}\xrightarrow {{U}}{\mathcal {C}} for the forgetful functor from the Eilenberg–Moore category \mathcal {C}^{\mathbb {T}}. If T preserves colimits of \mathcal {I}-figures for a given small category \mathcal {I}, then {\mathcal {C}^{\mathbb {T}}}\xrightarrow {{U}}{\mathcal {C}} creates colimits of \mathcal {I}-figures.

Let {\mathcal {I}}\xrightarrow {{X_\bullet }}{\mathcal {C}^{\mathbb {T}}} be a diagram such that \mathcal {I}\xrightarrow {X_\bullet }\mathcal {C}^{\mathbb {T}}\xrightarrow {U}\mathcal {C} has a (co)limit cocone {UX_\bullet }\xrightarrow {{c_\bullet }}{{\mathopen {}\left \{C\right \}\mathclose {}}}. Let \bar {C}={\mathopen {}\left (C,\alpha _{\bar {C}}\right )\mathclose {}} be the canonical algebra structure on the colimit apex C such that {UX_\bullet }\xrightarrow {{c_\bullet }}{{\mathopen {}\left \{C\right \}\mathclose {}}} lifts to a cocone of algebras. Then we conclude that this cocone is universal because U reflects colimits of \mathcal {I}-figures. It is evident (by definition) that U preserves this colimit.

fr:title.text: Algebra structure on the colimit apex

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Algebra structure on the colimit apex

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \mathcal {C} be a category and let \mathbb {T} = {\mathopen {}\left (T,\eta ,\mu \right )\mathclose {}} be a monad on \mathcal {C}, writing {\mathcal {C}^{\mathbb {T}}}\xrightarrow {{U}}{\mathcal {C}} for the forgetful functor from the Eilenberg–Moore category \mathcal {C}^{\mathbb {T}}. Suppose that T preserves colimits of \mathcal {I}-figures for a given small category \mathcal {I}. Let {\mathcal {I}}\xrightarrow {{X_\bullet }}{\mathcal {C}^{\mathbb {T}}} be a diagram in \mathcal {C}^{\mathbb {T}} such that \mathcal {I}\xrightarrow {X_\bullet }\mathcal {C}^{\mathbb {T}}\xrightarrow {U}\mathcal {C} has a universal cocone {UX_\bullet }\xrightarrow {{c_\bullet }}{{\mathopen {}\left \{C\right \}\mathclose {}}}. We may lift C to an essentially unique \mathbb {T}-algebra structure \bar {C} with U\bar {C}=C in a canonical way such that {UX_\bullet }\xrightarrow {{c_\bullet }}{{\mathopen {}\left \{C\right \}\mathclose {}}} lifts to a cocone of algebras {X_{\bullet }}\xrightarrow {{\bar {c}_{\bullet }}}{\bar {C}} with U\bar {c}_\bullet =c_\bullet .

By assumption, {TUX_\bullet }\xrightarrow {{Tc_\bullet }}{{\mathopen {}\left \{TC\right \}\mathclose {}}} is a universal cocone. We may define a further cocone TUX_\bullet \xrightarrow {\alpha _{X_\bullet }}UX_\bullet \xrightarrow {c_\bullet }{\mathopen {}\left \{C\right \}\mathclose {}}. The universal property of Tc_\bullet then gives us a unique morphism {TC}\xrightarrow {{\beta }}{C} satisfying the following condition:

fr:resource.fr:resource-source: \DiagramSquare { nw = TUX_\bullet , sw = UX_\bullet , ne = {\mathopen {}\left \{TC\right \}\mathclose {}}, se = {\mathopen {}\left \{C\right \}\mathclose {}}, west = \alpha _{X_\bullet }, south = c_\bullet , east = \exists !{\mathopen {}\left \{\beta \right \}\mathclose {}}, north = Tc_\bullet , east/style = {exists,->}, }

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: We must show that the map {TC}\xrightarrow {{\beta }}{C} depicted above satisfies the axioms of a \mathbb {T}-algebra.

fr:li.fr:p: To show that C\xrightarrow {\eta _C}TC\xrightarrow {\beta }C is the identity, we note that {UX_\bullet }\xrightarrow {{c_\bullet }}{{\mathopen {}\left \{C\right \}\mathclose {}}} is universal so it suffices to check that UX_\bullet \xrightarrow {c_\bullet }{\mathopen {}\left \{C\right \}\mathclose {}}\xrightarrow {{\mathopen {}\left \{\eta _C\right \}\mathclose {}}}{\mathopen {}\left \{TC\right \}\mathclose {}}\xrightarrow {{\mathopen {}\left \{\beta \right \}\mathclose {}}}{{\mathopen {}\left \{C\right \}\mathclose {}}} is equal to c_\bullet by the uniqueness property. This follows by inspection of the following commuting diagram, in which the top square is the naturality of the unit:
We must check that the the following multiplication square commutes:

fr:resource.fr:resource-source: \DiagramSquare { nw = TTC, ne = TC, sw = TC, se = C, east = \beta , south = \beta , north = T\beta , west = \mu _C, }

fr:li.fr:p: By assumption, we know that {TTUX_\bullet }\xrightarrow {{TTc_\bullet }}{{\mathopen {}\left \{TTC\right \}\mathclose {}}} is a universal cocone; using this fact as well as the naturality of \mu , it suffices to check that the following diagram commutes:

fr:resource.fr:resource-source: \begin {tikzpicture}[diagram] \SpliceDiagramSquare <l/>{ east/style = {draw=none}, nw = TTUX_\bullet , sw = TUX_\bullet , ne = {\mathopen {}\left \{TTC\right \}\mathclose {}}, se = {\mathopen {}\left \{TC\right \}\mathclose {}}, north = TTc_\bullet , south = Tc_\bullet , west = \mu _{UX_\bullet }, width = 2.5cm, } \SpliceDiagramSquare <r/>{ glue target = l/, glue = west, ne = {\mathopen {}\left \{TC\right \}\mathclose {}}, se = {\mathopen {}\left \{C\right \}\mathclose {}}, north = {\mathopen {}\left \{T\beta \right \}\mathclose {}}, east = {\mathopen {}\left \{\beta \right \}\mathclose {}}, south = {\mathopen {}\left \{\beta \right \}\mathclose {}}, width = 2.5cm, } \end {tikzpicture}

fr:li.fr:p: We compute algebraically, using the algebra property of X among other assumptions:

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Hence we may define a \mathbb {T}-algebra structure \bar {C} with U\bar {C}=C, setting {TC}\xrightarrow {{\alpha _{\bar {C}}}}{C} to be \beta . That c_\bullet lifts to a cocone of algebras is exactly the defining condition of \alpha _{\bar {C}}=\beta via the universal property of {TUX_\bullet }\xrightarrow {{Tc_\bullet }}{{\mathopen {}\left \{TC\right \}\mathclose {}}}; uniqueness of the algebra structure with this property follows from the same universal property.

fr:title.text: Reflection of colimits by the forgetful functor

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Reflection of colimits by the forgetful functor

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let \mathcal {C} be a category and let \mathbb {T} = {\mathopen {}\left (T,\eta ,\mu \right )\mathclose {}} be a monad on \mathcal {C}, writing {\mathcal {C}^{\mathbb {T}}}\xrightarrow {{U}}{\mathcal {C}} for the forgetful functor from the Eilenberg–Moore category \mathcal {C}^{\mathbb {T}}. If T preserves colimits of \mathcal {I}-figures for a given indexing category \mathcal {I}, then {\mathcal {C}^{\mathbb {T}}}\xrightarrow {{U}}{\mathcal {C}} reflects colimits of \mathcal {I}-figures.

Let {\mathcal {I}}\xrightarrow {{X_\bullet }}{\mathcal {C}^{\mathbb {T}}} be a diagram in \mathcal {C}^{\mathbb {T}} and let {X_\bullet }\xrightarrow {{y_\bullet }}{{\mathopen {}\left \{Y\right \}\mathclose {}}} be a cocone in \mathcal {C}^{\mathbb {T}} such that {UX_\bullet }\xrightarrow {{Uy_\bullet }}{{\mathopen {}\left \{UY\right \}\mathclose {}}} is a universal cocone for UX_\bullet = \mathcal {I}\xrightarrow {X_\bullet }\mathcal {C}^{\mathbb {T}}\xrightarrow {U}\mathcal {C}. We must show that {X_\bullet }\xrightarrow {{y_\bullet }}{{\mathopen {}\left \{Y\right \}\mathclose {}}} is a universal cocone.

In particular, we must factor any cocone {X_\bullet }\xrightarrow {{z_\bullet }}{{\mathopen {}\left \{Z\right \}\mathclose {}}} uniquely through {X_\bullet }\xrightarrow {{y_\bullet }}{{\mathopen {}\left \{Y\right \}\mathclose {}}}. Because Uy_\bullet is universal, there exists a unique {Y}\xrightarrow {{h}}{Z} such that the following diagram commutes:
We must check that h is a homomorphism of \mathbb {T}-algebras:

fr:resource.fr:resource-source: \DiagramSquare { nw = TUY, sw = TUZ, ne = UY, se = UZ, south = \alpha _Z, north = \alpha _Y, west = Th, east = h, }

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: To see that \alpha _Y;h = Th;\alpha _Z it suffices to observe that they both factor the cocone
through the cocone {TUX_\bullet }\xrightarrow {{TUy_\bullet }}{{\mathopen {}\left \{TUY\right \}\mathclose {}}}, which is universal by our assumption that T preserves \mathcal {I}-colimits.

fr:li.fr:p: First we must check that the following diagram commutes:

fr:resource.fr:resource-source: \DiagramSquare { nw = TUX_\bullet , sw = UX_\bullet , se = {\mathopen {}\left \{UZ\right \}\mathclose {}}, ne = {\mathopen {}\left \{TUY\right \}\mathclose {}}, north = TUy_\bullet , west = \alpha _{X_\bullet }, south = Uz_\bullet , east = \alpha _Y;h, width = 2.5cm, }

fr:li.fr:p: This holds immediately from the homomorphism property of y_\bullet .
Second, we must check that the following diagram commutes:

fr:resource.fr:resource-source: \DiagramSquare { nw = TUX_\bullet , sw = UX_\bullet , se = {\mathopen {}\left \{UZ\right \}\mathclose {}}, ne = {\mathopen {}\left \{TUY\right \}\mathclose {}}, north = TUy_\bullet , west = \alpha _{X_\bullet }, south = Uz_\bullet , east = Th;\alpha _Z, width = 2.5cm, }

fr:li.fr:p: This follows from the factorization Uy_\bullet ;h=Uz_\bullet and the homomorphism property of z_\bullet .
